mission.title: vault: Vault UI Support For TLS Cert Auth Method For Login
mission.description: The Vault CLI supports TLS certificate authentication for login, but the Vault UI did not provide equivalent support. Users with client certificates installed in their browser could not authenticate with Vault via the UI. Requested by 39+ users.

steps.title: Check current Vault setup
steps.description:
Verify your Vault version and check if the cert auth method is enabled:
```bash
vault version
vault auth list | grep cert
```
The TLS cert auth UI support requires a Vault version that includes the fix from PR #29089.

steps.title: Enable and configure TLS cert auth method
steps.description:
If not already enabled, set up the TLS certificate auth method:
```bash
# Enable the cert auth method:
vault auth enable cert

# Configure a certificate role with a trusted CA:
vault write auth/cert/certs/my-cert-role \
  display_name=my-cert \
  policies=default \
  certificate=@/path/to/ca-cert.pem
```
Make sure your Vault server is configured with TLS and that the client certificate's CA is trusted.

steps.title: Log in via the Vault UI using a client certificate
steps.description:
After upgrading to a Vault version with UI TLS cert auth support:
1. Install a client certificate in your browser
2. Navigate to the Vault UI login page
3. Select the **TLS Certificate** auth method from the login dropdown
4. The browser will prompt you to select a client certificate
5. Complete the login flow

Note: This does not work when running the Vault UI dev server (`yarn start`) due to the Ember proxy. For local testing, build with `make static-dist dev-ui`.

steps.title: Verify TLS cert authentication works via the UI
steps.description:
Confirm that:
1. The TLS Certificate option appears in the UI login method dropdown
2. Selecting it triggers the browser's certificate selection dialog
3. After selecting a valid client certificate, the user is authenticated and receives the correct policies
4. The Vault token is properly created with the expected TTL and policies

resolution.summary: The Vault UI now supports TLS certificate authentication for login. Users with client certificates installed in their browser can select the TLS Certificate auth method from the login dropdown, and the browser will handle the certificate selection. This provides parity with the Vault CLI's cert auth support.

prerequisites.description: A running Vault server with TLS enabled and the cert auth method configured.
